questions: Thread reply scope: does 'tweet thread reply' mean (a) replying to an *external* tweet the bot did not post (needs only an ID), (b) chaining a new tweet onto one the bot previously posted, or (c) both? I am assuming (c) — accepting any tweet ID/URL — because it is a strict superset.

For the admin-chat `reply_to_tweet` parameter, should the bot *require* media (current `post_tweet` requires attachments) or should thread replies be allowed text-only? I am assuming media is still required to keep the code path uniform; a text-only follow-up path would be a larger change.
Should the bot's own screen name be read from env (e.g. `TWITTER_BOT_HANDLE`) or from `verify_credentials()`? I am assuming `verify_credentials()` with a cache so there is no new config surface.
When a reply is posted, should the announcement channel message say 'Replied in thread: <url>' instead of 'Tweet: <url>'? I am assuming yes — minor label tweak in `_announce_tweet_url`.

success_criteria.criterion: post_tweet() accepts an optional in_reply_to_tweet_id and forwards it to tweepy's client_v2.create_tweet(in_reply_to_tweet_id=...)
Sharer.finalize_sharing() returns a dict containing tweet_url and tweet_id on success (instead of None) and a success=False dict on failure
Sharer.finalize_sharing() and Sharer.send_tweet() accept and forward in_reply_to_tweet_id down to post_tweet()
admin-chat share_to_social tool schema exposes a reply_to_tweet parameter (URL or ID), parses it into a numeric tweet ID, and forwards it to finalize_sharing
admin-chat execute_share_to_social returns tweet_url (and tweet_id) in its success response so the LLM can cite the posted tweet
Posting a standalone tweet still works end-to-end (no regression): media uploads, caption built, DB record written, announcement channel notified, first-share notification path unchanged
Returned tweet URL uses the bot's actual Twitter screen name rather than the hardcoded 'user' placeholder
Any existing tests touching post_tweet / finalize_sharing / execute_share_to_social continue to pass, and new tests cover the reply plumbing
No other callers of finalize_sharing are broken by its new return type (they currently ignore the return value)
Agent prompt (admin_chat/agent.py) mentions the new reply_to_tweet parameter and tweet_url return field
Manual smoke: post a standalone tweet, then post a reply that chains onto it in the Twitter UI as a thread

assumptions: 'Thread reply' means posting a new tweet whose in_reply_to_tweet_id points at an arbitrary target tweet — covers both replying to the bot's own prior tweet and replying to any external tweet.
Thread replies still require media attachments (same constraint as standalone tweets), since post_tweet already enforces this and loosening it is out of scope.
The admin-chat tool is the primary consumer that needs the returned URL; other finalize_sharing callers currently discard the return value and will continue to work with the new dict return type.
Twitter v2 API supports in_reply_to_tweet_id directly via tweepy.Client.create_tweet, so no OAuth scope changes are needed.
We'll cache the authenticated screen name via api_v1.verify_credentials() at first use rather than adding a TWITTER_BOT_HANDLE env var.
Tests exist or can be added with mocked tweepy clients; no live API calls are required for CI.
